_meta.purpose: PLACEHOLDER precommit file produced during N03 dev iteration. NOT a real EQ#1 submission.
_meta.note_to_future_self: Replace this file with the real student-produced precommit before generating any EQ#2 number that gets reported.

aggregation_level.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. PlantDoc has 8-12 test images per class, which is too small for reliable per-class accuracy claims. Aggregating to fungal/bacterial/viral/pest/healthy gives 5 categories with enough images per category to support claims, and the category is the conceptually meaningful unit for the transfer question: does a lab-trained model recognize 'this is a fungal disease' regardless of host?

class_space_remapping.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. Five categories. Fungal is the largest bucket (most PV diseases). Bacterial covers three entries: Citrus greening (Liberibacter), Peach bacterial spot, Pepper / Tomato bacterial spot. Viral covers two: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus and Tomato mosaic virus. Pest covers one: spider mites (arthropod feeding damage, not a microbial pathogen — categorized separately rather than dropped). Healthy covers all 12 healthy classes. Late blight (Phytophthora) is technically an oomycete, but grouped with fungal here at student-level resolution. Student would verify each row against their reading list before submitting; placeholder version trusts general plant-pathology references.

statistical_test.reasoning: PLACEHOLDER. Bootstrap confidence intervals around aggregate accuracy on PV-internal vs PV->PD. Two intervals that don't overlap -> distinguishable. Bootstrap is appropriate because the aggregate-accuracy distribution doesn't have an obvious parametric form. n=1000 resamples is plenty for stable CI estimates at the aggregate (5-category) level.
